title: Roll a release back to a known-good revision
description: Use helm history to pick the last healthy revision and helm rollback to restore it, then verify workload readiness.
body: Use when a release is unhealthy and you must restore service. Do not edit live objects or delete the release to recover. Actions: run helm history <release> to list revisions and statuses, identify the last deployed/healthy revision, run helm rollback <release> <revision> (optionally --wait), and confirm pods reach ready. Validation signals include helm history showing a new deployed revision pointing at the restored manifest and the workload passing readiness. Risk boundary: production rollback is change-controlled; record the revision and reason, and escalate if persistent state cannot revert with the release.
